terms.definition: The vector ∇f(x) of all partial derivatives of a scalar function f at point x; encodes local rate and direction of change in multivariable calculus and ML.
Synonym for gradient: the n-dimensional vector whose i-th component is ∂f/∂xᵢ, pointing toward steepest ascent when non-zero.
The ∇ symbol denoting the gradient operator; applied to a scalar field yields its gradient vector.
At x, the unit direction u that maximizes the directional derivative D_u f; aligned with ∇f(x) when ∇f(x) ≠ 0.
The unit direction that maximally decreases f locally; aligned with −∇f(x), used as the update direction in gradient-based minimization.
